mnemonics: Use the acronym C.P.R. for Capacity Planning Requirements: Capacity (maximum load), Performance (response time), and Resources (CPU, memory, storage, network).
keyPoints: Functional requirements define what the system must do, while non-functional requirements (like capacity and performance) define how well it must do it.
Measurements are quantifiable data points (e.g., CPU utilization %, transaction throughput) used to assess current performance and forecast future needs.
Baseline measurements establish a normal performance profile for a system, which is essential for identifying bottlenecks and planning for growth.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on peak capacity and ignoring average load, leading to over-provisioning and wasted resources.
Confusing throughput (work done per unit of time) with response time (how long a single operation takes).
Neglecting to plan for future growth, resulting in systems that quickly become obsolete and require emergency upgrades.
